candidates.label: Percolation theory × T cell infiltration
candidates.field_a: Statistical physics — percolation theory on random lattices
candidates.field_c: Tumor immunology — T cell exclusion by ECM

evidence_for_partial_exploration.paper: Ashworth et al. 2015, Adv Healthcare Mat (PMID 25881025)
evidence_for_partial_exploration.finding: Percolation theory applied to collagen scaffold interconnectivity and generic cell invasion. Site/pore percolation. Tissue engineering context, NOT tumor immunology.
evidence_for_partial_exploration.gap: Not T cells; not bond percolation on crosslinking network; not LOX as bond probability; not immune exclusion

evidence_for_partial_exploration.finding: LOX inhibition disrupts collagen crosslinking, reduces tumor stiffness, improves T cell infiltration and anti-PD-1 efficacy
evidence_for_partial_exploration.gap: No percolation or network-theoretic framework; no threshold analysis

evidence_for_partial_exploration.finding: Macrophages control fibrillar collagen topography (Tcf4-Col3 axis), dictating T cell localization

candidates.novel_bridge: Bond percolation threshold p_c = critical LOX crosslink density for immune exclusion. LOX activity = bond probability p. Correlation length exponent ν predicts spatial pattern of T cell exclusion near threshold. Anomalous diffusion of T cells on percolation clusters near p_c.
candidates.generator_note: Must acknowledge Ashworth 2015 and explain distinction: bond vs site percolation, T cells vs generic cells, tumor ECM vs engineered scaffold, LOX mechanism as bond probability

candidates.label: Acoustic filter-bank theory × Plant sound detection
candidates.field_a: Acoustic engineering — matched-filter detection and parallel filter-bank architectures
candidates.field_c: Plant bioacoustics — detecting airborne ultrasonic stress emissions via MSL channels
evidence_for_disjointness: No paper applies matched-filter theory to any plant acoustic detection mechanism
No paper analyzes MSL channel diversity as a parallel filter-bank architecture
Merdan & Akan 2025 (arXiv 2512.01096): first plant acoustic communication model — uses biological kinetic models, NOT filter-bank/matched-filter theory
MSL10 frequency response characterized at 1-30 Hz (PNAS 2021) but not in filter-bank framing
Plant ultrasound field is young (2019-2025); no acoustic engineering cross-pollination found
supporting_biology: Plant UEs: 20-150 kHz, peaks at 40-80 kHz (Bonisoli 2024)
MSL10 participates in perception of mechanical oscillations caused by wind (PNAS 2021)
MSL10 kinetics: frequency-dependent channel opening at 1 Hz, 3 Hz, 30 Hz
Multiple MSL paralogues (MSL2, 3, 9, 10) with different kinetic properties
Sound activates MCA2 → Ca²⁺ influx → CPK29 → auxin transport
candidates.novel_bridge: MSL2/3/9/10 channels as parallel filter-bank with different center frequencies and bandwidths. Trichome resonant frequency = bandpass filter center. Matched-filter SNR analysis for detecting intraspecific ultrasonic stress emissions. Ca²⁺ frequency-selective signaling as spectral output.
candidates.generator_note: Strong DISJOINT. Build on MSL10 kinetics (PNAS 2021) and multiple MSL paralogues as the physical basis for filter-bank. Calculate matched-filter SNR for detecting UEs from conspecifics.

candidates.label: Jamming phase diagram × Chromatin compaction
candidates.field_a: Granular physics — jamming transition, Liu-Nagel phase diagram, Edwards entropy
evidence_for_disjointness: Jamming applied to CELLS in tissues is well-explored; explicit statement found: 'Cell jamming theories consistently emphasize cell shapes rather than nucleus shapes'
Chromatin physics uses phase separation (LLPS) and active hydrodynamics — NOT granular jamming
Bajpai 2021 (eLife): chromatin phase separation — polymer model, no jamming
Rautu 2025 (arXiv 2503.20964): most advanced chromatin physics theory — active hydrodynamics, no jamming
Nature Comms 2025: chromatin condensate dynamics — no jamming
Extensive search for 'jamming chromatin Liu-Nagel nucleosome contact network' returned zero relevant hits
supporting_physics: HP1 bridging interactions increase with heterochromatin formation → analogous to increasing contact number z
Heterochromatin formation is relatively abrupt (threshold-like) — consistent with jamming transition
Force-chain-like mechanical stress transmission through heterochromatic regions observed but unmodeled
Nuclear tension changes during cell cycle — maps to 'applied stress' axis in Liu-Nagel diagram
candidates.novel_bridge: Liu-Nagel axes mapped to chromatin: density (nucleosome packing fraction φ), temperature (transcriptional activity / histone acetylation), stress (nuclear tension). z_c = critical HP1 bridging coordination number for heterochromatin jamming. Force chains transmit mechanical signals through compacted heterochromatin domains. Edwards entropy (compactivity) as measure of accessible chromatin configurations.
candidates.generator_note: Clear DISJOINT. Map Liu-Nagel axes explicitly onto chromatin parameters. Distinguish from LLPS: jamming is contact-driven, LLPS is interaction-driven — they apply at different scales and to different chromatin organizational transitions.

candidates.label: Thermodynamic uncertainty relation × Bacterial cell size homeostasis
candidates.field_a: Stochastic thermodynamics — TUR, entropy production bounds
candidates.field_c: Bacterial cell biology — cell size homeostasis via adder model
evidence_for_disjointness: TUR applied to: biochemical clocks (Marsland 2019), molecular motors, signaling (Verma 2025), active matter — NOT cell size homeostasis
Stochastic thermodynamics of cell size models (2025 paper) exists but uses general entropy production formalism, not TUR inequality (CV² × σ̇ × τ ≥ 2kT)
Adder model papers do not cite TUR or stochastic thermodynamics literature
DnaA-ATP as a TUR 'current' is not proposed in any paper
Growth rate as entropy production proxy for cell size precision: not found in literature

closest_existing_work.paper: Unnamed 2025 paper on stochastic thermodynamics of cell size models
closest_existing_work.finding: Applies entropy production analysis to sizer/timer/adder models; finds precision-metabolism trade-off; entropy production of resetting vs. branching
closest_existing_work.gap: Uses general entropy production formalism; does NOT apply TUR inequality; does NOT identify DnaA-ATP as the molecular counter; does NOT connect to nutrient-dependent growth rate as dissipation proxy

candidates.novel_bridge: TUR bound: CV²_added × σ̇ × τ_generation ≥ 2kT. Growth rate μ as entropy production rate proxy (σ̇ ∝ μ). DnaA-ATP hydrolysis as the irreversible current whose CV sets the size precision. Nutrient-rich conditions: higher μ → higher dissipation → tighter CV bound predicts higher size precision. Prediction: CV_added vs. growth rate should follow TUR-bounded curve, testable with mother-machine + metabolic reporters.
candidates.generator_note: CLEANEST DISJOINT. Highest priority for hypothesis generation. Both fields are mature and quantitative. The bridge is mechanistically tight (DnaA-ATP as molecular current) and falsifiable (nutrient-dependent CV vs. σ̇ relationship). No near-miss papers found. Generator should specify the TUR bound explicitly and map each component to bacterial biology.

candidates.label: FLIM metabolic imaging × Bacterial persisters
candidates.field_a: Optical metabolic imaging — FLIM NADH/FAD phasor analysis
candidates.field_c: Antimicrobial resistance — persister cell metabolic transitions

evidence_for_partial_exploration.finding: FLIM phasor metabolic fingerprinting of 5 bacterial species; NADH autofluorescence; single-cell resolution
evidence_for_partial_exploration.gap: Not specifically persisters; batch cultures, not mother-machine; endpoint snapshots, not longitudinal tracking

evidence_for_partial_exploration.finding: Rapid antibiotic susceptibility testing by FLIM metabolic tracking; differentiates susceptible/resistant in 10 min
evidence_for_partial_exploration.gap: Addresses antibiotic phenotype broadly, not persister transition specifically; no mother-machine; endpoint, not longitudinal

evidence_for_partial_exploration.finding: FLIM detects long-lifetime signal associated with reduced pyocyanin in P. aeruginosa biofilms
evidence_for_partial_exploration.gap: Biofilm context, not individual persister cells; no persister transition tracking

candidates.novel_bridge: Phasor-FLIM + mother-machine microfluidics for real-time longitudinal tracking of single-cell persister transition. Pre-persister NADH lifetime signature (predictive marker hours before commitment). Phasor trajectory showing the commitment point. Distinction from existing work: real-time tracking of the stochastic TRANSITION event, not endpoint characterization.
candidates.generator_note: PARTIALLY_EXPLORED — Scout's flag confirmed. Must explicitly acknowledge prior art (Bhattacharjee 2017, ACS 2024). Hypothesis strength lies in: (1) longitudinal real-time tracking vs. endpoint, (2) mother-machine enabling observation of rare stochastic events, (3) pre-persister predictive signature. Scorer will penalize if prior art not acknowledged.

candidates.label: Classical nucleation theory × Ferroptosis iron release
candidates.field_a: Physical chemistry — CNT, Gibbs-Thomson, Ostwald ripening
candidates.field_c: Ferroptosis biology — ferritin to labile iron pool transition
evidence_for_disjointness: 2025 JACS papers on ferritin nucleation exist completely separate from ferroptosis literature
Ferroptosis reviews (Signal Transduction 2024, Frontiers 2025) describe iron sources without nucleation physics
Ponnusamy 2025: LIP does NOT expand during ferroptosis induction — mechanism unexplained, opens CNT gap
No paper applies CNT, Gibbs-Thomson, or Ostwald ripening to ferritin iron release in ferroptosis context
Ferritin L subunit as 'nucleation center' noted in biochemistry literature but not developed into CNT framework
candidates.key_anomaly: Ponnusamy 2025 shows LIP does NOT measurably increase during pharmacological ferroptosis induction — challenging the dominant model. CNT provides a potential explanation: ferritin dissolution is a kinetically regulated process (dissolution rate depends on pH, supersaturation, core size distribution), not a simple release. Ostwald ripening between ferritin cores of different sizes could create a buffer against LIP expansion.
supporting_chemistry: JACS 2025 (mini-ferritin): nucleation at acidic 3-fold pores; Ostwald ripening of nascent mineral from nucleation site to core
JACS 2025 (mitochondrial ferritin): cryo-EM of mineral core assembly at nucleation site
Lysosomal pH shift (7.4 → 4.5) during ferritinophagy changes iron solubility dramatically — dissolution kinetics should follow rate law
candidates.novel_bridge: Ferrihydrite dissolution from ferritin under lysosomal acidification modeled as reverse nucleation. Gibbs-Thomson effect: smaller ferritin cores dissolve faster than larger ones (size-dependent solubility). Ostwald ripening between multiple ferritin cores: iron released from small cores re-precipitates on large cores → explains why LIP doesn't accumulate as expected. Critical pH for dissolution threshold = nucleation/dissolution rate crossover.
candidates.generator_note: Strong DISJOINT. The Ponnusamy 2025 anomaly (LIP doesn't expand) is an open question that CNT directly addresses. The JACS 2025 ferritin nucleation papers establish the physical chemistry foundation. High impact if correct: mechanistic explanation of a paradox in ferroptosis biology using physical chemistry.

disjointness_ranking.rationale: Cleanest gap, both fields maximally quantitative, mechanistically specific, no near-miss papers
Complete separation; Ponnusamy 2025 anomaly creates perfect entry point; JACS 2025 papers give physical chemistry foundation
Highest confidence but plant bioacoustics is a younger field; MSL10 kinetics give empirical support
Strong gap but active matter/phase separation literature is adjacent and growing; distinction from LLPS must be made
Ashworth 2015 precedent; must distinguish bond vs site percolation and T cell specificity
Multiple FLIM+bacteria papers exist; hypothesis is narrow (longitudinal phasor-FLIM+mother-machine)
